external_framework_alignment.implication: Treat governance, measurement, managed risk, and critical-infrastructure trust evidence as proof gates, not marketing copy.
external_framework_alignment.missing_proof: Real resolved outcomes, comparable baselines, hosted/runtime monitoring evidence, RLS proof, and owner-approved claim language.
external_framework_alignment.claim_boundary: Supports governed pilot framing only; does not prove trustworthy deployed AI management or world-class prediction accuracy.

external_framework_alignment.implication: Enterprise procurement should see repeatable secure-development, vulnerability, and acquisition evidence rather than broad security claims.
external_framework_alignment.current_evidence: enterprise_execution_ready_for_owner_trust_review=true; RLS executed rows=0/54; ready procurement documents=0/8.
external_framework_alignment.missing_proof: Owner-approved procurement documents, RLS pgTAP/local/linked execution, hosted smoke evidence, incident/support/privacy controls, and external-share approvals.
external_framework_alignment.claim_boundary: Do not claim enterprise-ready or secure-development maturity from static repo evidence alone.

external_framework_alignment.framework: CISA Secure by Design and Secure by Demand
external_framework_alignment.implication: Use supplier security questions to separate current proof, missing proof, owner actions, and buyer-safe claims.
external_framework_alignment.missing_proof: Owner-completed supplier questionnaire, hosted proof, redacted logs, RLS evidence, AI runtime policy approval, and no-secrets external-share review.
external_framework_alignment.claim_boundary: Buyer security review can be prepared; buyer-facing security claims stay blocked until owner/runtime proof exists.

external_framework_alignment.implication: LLM security proof must cover prompt injection, sensitive information disclosure, output handling, supply chain, excessive agency, and unbounded consumption.
external_framework_alignment.current_evidence: LLM local fixtures passed=10/10; hosted runtime fixtures executed=0; AI hosted boundary tests=0.
external_framework_alignment.missing_proof: Owner-approved hosted LLM red-team smoke, hosted no-autonomous-action tests, redacted logs, and approved high-impact action policy.
external_framework_alignment.claim_boundary: Local/static security proof can support internal readiness only; hosted/public-sector LLM security claims remain blocked.

external_framework_alignment.implication: AI governance can be mapped for readiness, but certification or an audited AI management system cannot be claimed from repo artifacts alone.
external_framework_alignment.missing_proof: Audited AI management-system scope, risk register, accountable owners, monitoring evidence, document control, and certification/audit evidence if any certification is claimed.
external_framework_alignment.claim_boundary: Do not imply ISO certification, audited AI management system, or enterprise AI governance maturity.

external_framework_alignment.implication: Worldwide public-sector and enterprise buyers need clear risk classification, human oversight, logging, accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity, and high-impact action boundaries.
external_framework_alignment.current_evidence: AI action inventory ready=true; AI action policy ready=true; owner-approved policies=0; hosted verified tests=0.
external_framework_alignment.missing_proof: Owner-approved AI action policy, hosted no-autonomous-action tests, logging/monitoring evidence, human oversight proof, and jurisdiction-specific legal review before regulated-market claims.
external_framework_alignment.claim_boundary: Supports internal risk-control planning only; not EU AI Act compliance proof.

external_framework_alignment.implication: Marketing claims about AI capability, superiority, accuracy, replacement, or readiness must be substantiated before they are shown externally.
external_framework_alignment.current_evidence: unsupported claim mentions=0; scanned files=153; claim_consistency_ready=true.
external_framework_alignment.missing_proof: Owner-approved external claim language plus buyer validation, hosted proof, enterprise proof, and real forecast scoring before stronger market claims.
external_framework_alignment.claim_boundary: Use governed pilot language; block world-class, accuracy, replacement, hosted-live, buyer-validated, and enterprise-ready claims.

external_framework_alignment.implication: Forecasting claims need resolved outcomes and comparison against human/community/pro or model baselines.
external_framework_alignment.current_evidence: valid/real resolved outcomes=0/0; valid/real baselines=0/0; scoring_chain_ready=false.
external_framework_alignment.missing_proof: Owner-approved resolved forecast export, comparable baselines, leakage review, calibration ledger, benchmark comparison, and claim-governance approval.
external_framework_alignment.claim_boundary: Forecasting mechanics are present; world-class or benchmark-superiority prediction claims remain blocked.

external_framework_alignment.implication: World-class AI forecasting claims require dynamic, contamination-resistant benchmarks and human accuracy references.
external_framework_alignment.missing_proof: Dynamic/future-event benchmark set, contamination/leakage controls, comparable human/community/pro baselines, sufficient sample size, and owner-approved external claim language.
external_framework_alignment.claim_boundary: Sample fixtures and static benchmark mechanics cannot support world-class forecasting claims.

external_framework_alignment.implication: Prediction-model claims should report intended use, data provenance, participants/events, predictors, model versioning, performance, uncertainty, limitations, and external validation.
external_framework_alignment.current_evidence: protocol stages=8; metrics=6; claim tiers=4; real resolved outcomes=0.
external_framework_alignment.missing_proof: Real validation cohort/question set, external validation or comparable benchmark, performance uncertainty, data provenance, limitations, and owner-approved claim review.
external_framework_alignment.claim_boundary: Used as reporting rigor guidance only; not a direct domain certification for geopolitical or strategic forecasts.
